title: BMS P-1075 safety parameter update: SOC floor 18%, SOC ceiling 82%, temperature de-rating
text:
Engineering Change Request ECR-209.

Raised by: Lena Okafor (Battery & Power Systems Engineer), 2023-07-10.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-07-18.

Summary: Update BMS module (P-1075) safety parameters: SOC lower limit set to 18%, SOC upper limit set to 82%, and temperature de-rating curve updated per March 2023 bench test data.

Rationale: The March 2023 bench test series (internal reference BT-2023-03-12) conducted ahead of the DOC-431 BMS Safety Specification release identified two critical safety limits (discussed at WIKI-014, BMS Safety Architecture Review, 2023-07-01):

1. SOC Floor (18%): Below 18% SOC under 3C discharge, one of six series cells showed 12mV cell reversal for ~40 seconds. Cell reversal leads to separator failure and thermal event risk. The 18% floor provides a 6-point margin above the 12% reversal-onset, validated across the operating temperature range (-5°C to +45°C) by thermal modelling.

2. SOC Ceiling (82%): At SOC above 85% with charge rate above 0.5C and ambient below 5°C, localised lithium plating was observed on anode graphite surfaces (confirmed by post-mortem optical microscopy). The 82% ceiling provides a 3-point margin below the 85% plating-onset. This limit is implemented in hardware on P-1075 to prevent bypass by any single-point software fault — per the decision at WIKI-014.

3. Temperature de-rating curve: The previous de-rating curve in DOC-431 §5.2 was based on manufacturer data only. The bench test data shows that at -5°C ambient the maximum allowable charge rate must be reduced to 0.3C (previously specified at 0.5C). DOC-431 §5.2 de-rating table is to be updated.

Dr. Rhea Voss's review of DOC-431 (REV-623) contains additional commentary on the safety rationale for these limits — this commentary must be preserved in DOC-431.

Impact:
- P-1075: Hardware limits updated (18% floor lockout, 82% ceiling lockout, new de-rating curve in EEPROM)
- DOC-431: §4.3 SOC limits and §5.2 de-rating table to be updated
